name: Legal Contract Review
description: Reviews a contract, flags risks, and generates a redlined version with negotiation memo

nodes.name: Parse Contract
nodes.instructions: Read the contract PDF. Extract every clause as a structured object with: clause number, title, full text, clause type (indemnification, limitation of liability, IP assignment, termination, non-compete, confidentiality, governing law, force majeure, other). Identify all defined terms. Write the parsed output.

nodes.name: Risk Analysis
nodes.instructions: Coordinate 3 parallel research subagents analyzing different aspects of the contract. Each writes its own findings file.

children.name: Liability & Indemnification Analyst
children.instructions: Review all indemnification, limitation of liability, warranty, and insurance clauses. For each: assess if terms are one-sided, identify missing protections (mutual indemnification, liability caps, carve-outs), compare against standard market terms. Flag clauses rated HIGH risk (uncapped liability, broad indemnification without mutual terms).

children.name: IP & Confidentiality Analyst
children.instructions: Review all intellectual property, work-for-hire, assignment, confidentiality, and non-compete clauses. Check: Does IP assignment include pre-existing IP carve-out? Are confidentiality obligations mutual? Is the non-compete scope reasonable (geography, duration, scope)? Flag overly broad IP transfers or one-sided confidentiality.

children.name: Termination & Governance Analyst
children.instructions: Review termination, renewal, governing law, dispute resolution, force majeure, and assignment clauses. Check: Are termination rights balanced? Is there adequate notice period? Is the governing law favorable? Is arbitration vs litigation specified? Are assignment restrictions reasonable? Flag auto-renewal without notice or unfavorable dispute resolution.

nodes.name: Attorney Review
nodes.instructions: Present the risk analysis to the reviewing attorney. Show HIGH and MEDIUM risk items with recommended changes. Ask for direction on each flagged clause: accept as-is, request specific change, or flag for partner review.

nodes.name: Generate Deliverables
nodes.instructions: Using the risk analysis and attorney decisions, generate three deliverables: (1) A redlined contract with tracked changes for each accepted modification, (2) A negotiation memo summarizing the position on each flagged clause with supporting rationale, (3) A risk summary table for the deal team.
